title: Understand: seasons
description: Check your understanding of seasons in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

choices.content: changes in Earth’s tilt relative to the sun
changes in Earth’s distance from the sun
Earth’s changing distance from the sun
Earth’s changing tilt relative to the sun
the sun getting hotter and colder
Earth getting closer to and farther from the sun
Earth’s axis tilting toward or away from the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the Northern Hemisphere experience summer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when it points toward the sun
when it points away from the sun
when it points neither toward nor away from the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the Northern Hemisphere experience winter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when it points toward the sun
when it points away from the sun
when it points neither toward nor away from the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the Southern Hemisphere experience winter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when it points toward the sun
when it points away from the sun
when it points neither toward nor away from the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which regions on Earth receive the most direct sunlight throughout the year?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which regions on Earth receive the least direct sunlight throughout the year?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which regions on Earth receive the most direct sunlight throughout the year?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the regions between the equator and the poles

assessment_items.question_content:
**When it is winter in the Southern Hemisphere, what season is it in the Northern Hemisphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To solve this problem, remember that the Southern and Northern Hemispheres have *opposite* seasons.
This means that when it is winter in the Southern Hemisphere, it must be summer in the Northern Hemisphere.
So, the correct answer is summer.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When it is summer in the Southern Hemisphere, what season is it in the Northern Hemisphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To solve this problem, remember that the Southern and Northern Hemispheres have *opposite* seasons.
This means that when it is summer in the Southern Hemisphere, it must be winter in the Northern Hemisphere.
So, the correct answer is winter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When it is winter in the Northern Hemisphere, what season is it in the Southern Hemisphere?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To solve this problem, remember that the Northern and Southern Hemispheres have *opposite* seasons.
This means that when it is winter in the Northern Hemisphere, it must be summer in the Southern Hemisphere.
So, the correct answer is summer.
